contacts.name: Carson Law Group (AOR for R. Haines)

emails.subject: Welcome Back – April Kick-Off Memo
emails.body_text:
Team,

Happy April! A few reminders as we head into Q2:

1. All appeal acknowledgment letters must go out within 5 business days of receipt — no exceptions.
2. The new external review tracking spreadsheet is live in SharePoint. Please log all Case #s by EOD Friday.
3. Our next team huddle is Thursday, April 9, at 10:00 AM in Conference Room B.

Let's have a great quarter.

Tamra Reyes
Director, Appeals & Grievances
Mojave Crest Assurance Company

emails.subject: 🎂 Happy Birthday, Priya!
emails.body_text:
Hi team,

Please join us in wishing Priya Annadurai a very happy birthday today! There will be cupcakes in the break room at 2:00 PM.

HR Team
Mojave Crest Assurance Company

emails.subject: Action Required: Reset Your MCA Network Password
emails.body_text:
Hi Ari,

Your Mojave Crest network password will expire in 14 days. Please reset it before April 17 to avoid losing access.

To reset: go to https://myaccount.mojavecrest.com and click "Change Password."

If you need assistance, contact the IT Help Desk at ext. 5555 or helpdesk@mojavecrest.com.

IT Department
Mojave Crest Assurance Company

emails.subject: Appeal Submission – Robert Haines / QTR583920174
emails.body_text:
To Whom It May Concern:

Carson Law Group submits this formal appeal on behalf of our client, Robert Haines (Member ID: QTR583920174), regarding the denial of pre-service authorization for a lumbar spinal fusion (CPT 22612) dated March 28, 2026. The denial cited lack of medical necessity under MCA Medical Policy MP-SPINE-04.

Enclosed please find:
  - Signed Authorized Representative designation
  - Treating physician's letter of medical necessity (Dr. S. Park, MD)
  - Operative and diagnostic imaging reports

We request an expedited review given the member's functional limitations.

Sincerely,
Carson Law Group
Phone: (702) 555-0991

emails.subject: Quick Q – Case routing for spinal auth denials
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Hey – for the new spinal auth denial appeals coming in, are you routing those to Helena or straight to the panel? I want to make sure we're consistent across the team.

Also, did Carson Law's submission for Haines come through to you? I see it cc'd you.

Thanks,
Darnell

emails.subject: Insurance Appeal – Caldwell / LXM104857293
emails.body_text:
Hello,

My name is James Caldwell, Member ID LXM104857293. I received an Explanation of Benefits dated March 30, 2026, showing a claim for my colonoscopy on March 12 was processed as out-of-network, but my gastroenterologist is listed in-network on your website. I was charged $1,840 that I should not owe.

I am appealing this claim processing decision. Please let me know what else you need.

Thank you,
James Caldwell
(702) 555-3867

emails.subject: AHIP This Week: CMS Proposed Rule Highlights & State Round-Up
emails.body_text:
AHIP This Week | April 8, 2026

• CMS releases proposed rule on prior authorization timelines — comment period open through May 20
• Nevada Division of Insurance issues market conduct guidance on grievance turnaround times
• Federal court upholds ACA Section 1557 nondiscrimination protections
• New AHIP webinar: Managing External Review Obligations Under State Law — April 22, 1 PM ET

emails.subject: Formal Appeal – Darcy Okonkwo, Member ID ZPN672491058
emails.body_text:
Hello,

I am writing to appeal the denial of my outpatient intensive psychiatric program (IOP). Your denial letter dated April 4, 2026 stated the treatment was not medically necessary. My psychiatrist, Dr. Renata Miles, disagrees and has submitted documentation supporting the medical necessity of IOP following my recent inpatient stay.

Member: Darcy Okonkwo
Member ID: ZPN672491058
Date of Denial: April 4, 2026
Service: Outpatient IOP – Behavioral Health

Please review at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,
Darcy Okonkwo

emails.subject: Mojave Crest Appeals Case #CASE-2026-00695 - Acknowledgment of Your Appeal
emails.body_text:
Dear Darcy Okonkwo,

This letter confirms that Mojave Crest Assurance Company has received your appeal regarding the denial of outpatient intensive psychiatric program (IOP) services.

Your case details:
  Case Number: CASE-2026-00695
  Member Name: Darcy Okonkwo
  Member ID: ZPN672491058
  Date Received: April 11, 2026
  Appeal Type: Pre-Service / Behavioral Health
  Decision Due By: May 11, 2026

I will be your Appeals Coordinator throughout this case. I may contact you if additional information is needed to complete the review. You or your Authorized Representative may submit additional information in support of your appeal at any time before the decision date.

If you have questions about your case, please reply to this email or call the Appeals Unit at (702) 555-0148, referencing your Case Number above.

Sincerely,
Ari Costa
Appeals & Grievances Coordinator
Mojave Crest Assurance Company
Case #: CASE-2026-00695

emails.subject: Case 695 – parity flag?
emails.body_text:
Ari,

For the Okonkwo IOP appeal (695) – is that being flagged for parity review? The original denial was based on medical necessity but if the same criteria wouldn't apply to a comparable med/surg condition, Ayla will want to loop in the parity committee early.

Let me know where you land.

Darnell

Nevada Association of Health Plans | April 2026 Newsletter

emails.subject: Appeal of Claim Denial – Theresa Montoya
emails.body_text:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am appealing the partial denial of my claim for physical therapy services rendered in February and March 2026. My plan approved 12 sessions but my therapist says I need 20 for my rotator cuff repair recovery. The additional 8 sessions were denied as "exceeding plan limits."

I believe this limit is being applied incorrectly per my Evidence of Coverage. My PT notes are attached.

Member Name: Theresa Montoya
Member ID: KDV938205761
Dates of Service: Feb 3 – Mar 28, 2026

Please advise.

Theresa Montoya
(702) 555-2204

emails.subject: Scheduled Maintenance: ClaimsIQ Portal – Sunday Apr 19, 12–4 AM
emails.body_text:
Hi Ari Costa,

ClaimsIQ will be undergoing scheduled maintenance on Sunday, April 19 from 12:00 AM to 4:00 AM PST. During this window, the portal and API will be unavailable.

No action is required on your part. If you experience issues after 4:00 AM, contact support@claimsiq.io.

Thank you,
ClaimsIQ Platform Team

emails.subject: Re: Case load – can you take 705?
emails.body_text:
Ari,

I'm at capacity this week with the Vickers expedited and two parity reviews. Darnell approved me to offload CASE-2026-00705 (Durand – denied DME request). Are you able to pick it up? File is in SharePoint under Appeals Active. Deadline is May 10.

Let me know!
Priya

emails.subject: Re: External Review Request – Case #CASE-2026-00689 – STANDARD
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Thank you – we have received and logged the external review request for Case #CASE-2026-00689 (Member: R.H.). Our file has been assigned reference number CRR-2026-04-1188. We confirm receipt of the case file via secure transfer.

Expected decision: within 45 calendar days (by June 3, 2026).

Please route any supplemental materials to the same secure transfer portal.

Clear Skies,
Canyon Ridge Review Services Intake Team

emails.subject: External Review Request - Case #CASE-2026-00689 - STANDARD
emails.body_text:

Mojave Crest Assurance Company is forwarding the following case for independent external review:

  Case Number: CASE-2026-00689

emails.subject: Re: Mojave Crest Appeals Case #CASE-2026-00698 – Additional PT Records
emails.body_text:
Hi Ari,

Thank you for your acknowledgment. My physical therapist's office just faxed over my complete treatment notes and a letter of medical necessity. Can you confirm you received them? They said they sent it to (702) 555-0199.

Also, my EOC says I'm entitled to "medically necessary" therapy without a set visit cap under Section 9.4. Can I include that in my appeal?

Thank you,
Theresa

emails.subject: Q1 2026 Appeals Metrics – Please Review Before Huddle
emails.body_text:
Team,

Attached are the Q1 2026 appeals metrics for your review ahead of our huddle. Key items:

• Average acknowledgment turnaround: 3.1 days (target: ≤5) ✓
• Average decision turnaround: 22.4 days (target: ≤30) ✓
• External review rate: 14% (up from 9% in Q4 2025 — we should discuss)
• Parity-flagged cases: 6 (all resolved within period)

Please come to the huddle prepared to discuss your open case queues.

Tamra

emails.subject: Appeal Request – Ethan Brockman / HRC751093482
emails.body_text:
Hello,

I was denied coverage for a continuous glucose monitor (CGM) prescribed by my endocrinologist, Dr. V. Shah. The denial letter said CGMs are not covered under my plan for Type 2 diabetes, only Type 1. I believe this is incorrect based on the plan documents I was given when I enrolled.

Member: Ethan Brockman
Member ID: HRC751093482
Prescribing provider: Dr. V. Shah, MD
Device: Dexcom G7 CGM

Please help.

Ethan Brockman

emails.subject: CASE-2026-00701 – CGM coverage clarification
emails.body_text:
Ari,

For the Brockman CGM case (701), Jules and I are looking at the EOC language together. The 2025 EOC addendum actually expanded DME coverage for CGMs to members with insulin-requiring Type 2 diabetes — Dr. Shah's notes confirm the member is on basal insulin. This looks like it should be approved on appeal.

I'll have my formal clinical review memo to you by tomorrow afternoon.

Helena

emails.subject: Re: Mojave Crest Appeals Case #CASE-2026-00689 – External Review Status
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Thank you for forwarding the case to Canyon Ridge Review Services. Our client, Mr. Haines, would like to submit an additional letter from his physiatrist, Dr. M. Kaur, documenting his failed conservative treatments. Can we send this directly to Canyon Ridge, or does it go through you?

Also, can you confirm the expected decision date?

Thank you,
Carson Law Group

emails.subject: Office HVAC Maintenance – Floor 3, Wed Apr 30, 8–10 AM
emails.body_text:
All Staff – Floor 3,

Routine HVAC maintenance is scheduled for Wednesday, April 30, from 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM. During this time, expect some noise and possible temperature fluctuation in offices 304–318.

If you need to step out during this window, the 2nd floor breakout rooms are available.

Thank you for your patience.

Facilities Management

emails.subject: Case 698 – Montoya – clock check
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Just checking in – where are we on Montoya (698)? Due date is May 15 but I'd like the decision out by May 9 if possible given Marisol is out that week. Has Helena's review come in yet?

Also – did you get the Caldwell corrected EOB confirmation from Claims? Marin should have sent it yesterday.

Darnell

emails.subject: May Kickoff – Caseload, Staffing & Compliance Reminder
emails.body_text:
Team,

Happy May! A few things:

1. Marisol will be out May 5–9 for vacation. Clinical reviews during that period should be routed to Dr. Bergstrom or Dr. Ontario as appropriate.
2. The NV DOI market conduct exam window opens June 1 – please make sure all case files in SharePoint are complete and dated correctly.
3. Reminder: if a member requests external review on a partially approved case, the denied portion must be forwarded to Canyon Ridge within 5 business days.

Great work in April, everyone.

Tamra

emails.subject: Re: Mojave Crest Appeals Case #CASE-2026-00695 – I Want External Review
emails.body_text:
Hi Ari,

I received your letter about my appeal being denied. I disagree with the decision and want to request the independent external review mentioned in your letter.

Please proceed with sending my case to the independent review organization.

Thank you,
Darcy Okonkwo

emails.subject: URGENT – Expedited Appeal – Noah Vickers / WFA620483915
emails.body_text:
URGENT – EXPEDITED REVIEW REQUESTED

My name is Noah Vickers, Member ID WFA620483915. I am currently receiving inpatient rehabilitation services at Desert Valley Rehab Center following a stroke on April 18. My insurance just informed me that coverage will be terminated effective May 4 because I've reached 60 inpatient rehab days. My physiatrist says I am not yet medically stable enough for discharge and that stopping now would seriously jeopardize my health.

I am requesting an expedited concurrent care appeal. Please contact me or my wife Renee Vickers at (702) 555-7791.

Noah Vickers
WFA620483915

emails.body_text:
Ari,

The Parity Committee completed its review of CASE-2026-00695. The final memo is in SharePoint. Summary: parity analysis confirmed our criteria for IOP are comparable to med/surg sub-acute placement criteria; the case file is compliant. The denial stands on clinical grounds.

Since I see you've already issued the decision and forwarded to Canyon Ridge, this is just for the file record.

Ayla Ontario, MD
Medical Director, Behavioral Health

emails.subject: Member Appeal – Priscilla Ybarra / BJS849201376
emails.body_text:
Hello,

I am appealing the denial of my Humira (adalimumab) prescription. The denial says a step-therapy requirement applies and I must try methotrexate first. I tried methotrexate for 6 months in 2024 and it was ineffective and caused liver toxicity. My rheumatologist has documented this.

Member: Priscilla Ybarra
Member ID: BJS849201376
Prescribing Provider: Dr. C. Torres, MD (Rheumatology)
Medication: Humira 40mg/0.8mL pen

Please consider my appeal.

Priscilla Ybarra

emails.subject: External Review Decision – Case #CASE-2026-00689 / CRR-2026-04-1188
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Canyon Ridge Review Services has issued its independent external review decision for Case #CASE-2026-00689 (CRR ref: CRR-2026-04-1188).

External Review Decision: UPHOLD (MCA denial affirmed)

Summary: The independent reviewing physician concurred that the submitted clinical record does not demonstrate exhaustion of conservative therapies as required for surgical coverage. The formal decision letter has been mailed to the member and to MCA Compliance.

This case is now closed at the external review level.

Canyon Ridge Review Services

emails.subject: Cases 701 and 715 – status check
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Two quick questions:

1. Case 701 (Brockman CGM) – Helena sent you a clinical memo last week, right? What's the timeline on a decision? Jules flagged it as a potential approval.

2. Case 715 (Ybarra, Humira) – Jules is reviewing the step therapy exemption request now. If methotrexate failure and toxicity are documented from 2024, that should qualify under the Nevada step therapy exemption law. Keep an eye on Jules's review.

Thanks,
Darnell

emails.subject: NEW POLICY: Updated Expedited Review Turnaround SLA – Effective June 1
emails.body_text:
Team,

Effective June 1, 2026, our internal SLA for expedited appeals decisions is reduced from 72 hours to 48 hours from the time of receipt. This aligns with the new NV DOI guidance issued last month.

Please make note of this for any cases received on or after June 1. I'll be updating the Appeals Procedures Manual this week.

Questions? See me or Darnell.

Tamra Reyes
Director, Appeals & Grievances
Mojave Crest Assurance Company

emails.subject: Re: Mojave Crest Appeals Case #CASE-2026-00698 – Still disputing sessions 19-20
emails.body_text:
Hi Ari,

Thank you for the partial approval on my appeal. I'm glad sessions 13–18 were approved, but I still feel sessions 19 and 20 were medically necessary. My PT says I was not at functional goals at session 18.

Do I need to appeal again, or can I go straight to the external review you mentioned in your letter?

Thank you,
Theresa Montoya

emails.subject: Re: Appeal – Ethan Brockman / CASE-2026-00701 – CGM Decision
emails.body_text:

Thank you so much! I received your approval letter today. I'm so relieved. I'll share it with Dr. Shah's office right away.

Compliance Officer

emails.subject: Dr. Henning – Step Therapy Exemption Review / Case 715
emails.body_text:
Ari,

I've completed my pharmacy review for Case 715 (Ybarra). The 2024 medical records documenting methotrexate failure and hepatotoxicity are clear and meet the Nevada step therapy exemption criteria (NRS 695G.210). My recommendation is to approve the Humira authorization. Full memo will be in SharePoint by EOD.

Jules Henning, PharmD
Pharmacy Director

emails.body_text:

emails.subject: Re: CASE-2026-00698 – Montoya – Marisol clinical review attached
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Here is my clinical review summary for Case 698 (Montoya):

Sessions 13–18 are clinically supported. The PT notes show the member was still working toward range-of-motion and strength goals consistent with her post-op protocol at session 12. Sessions 19–20: the progress notes at session 18 show the member had reached 90% of functional benchmarks with plateau noted. I cannot support sessions 19–20 on the current record.

My full memo is in SharePoint. Feel free to call if you have questions before drafting the letter.

Marisol Alvarado, RN
Lead RN Reviewer

emails.subject: Health Plan Week – Concurrent Care & Step Therapy Updates
emails.body_text:
Health Plan Week | May 9, 2026

• Nevada amends concurrent care termination notice requirements – 10-day notice now required for non-expedited terminations
• Step therapy exemption laws: state comparison chart updated for 2026
• CMS posts final rule on behavioral health network adequacy — effective January 2027
• Webinar: Preparing for state market conduct exams – May 19, 12 PM ET

Read more at healthplanweek.com.

emails.subject: Re: Case 693 – Caldwell – Thank You
emails.body_text:
Hi Ari,

Thank you so much for resolving this. I received confirmation that the claim was reprocessed. I'll be watching for the refund check. I really appreciate how quickly this was handled.

Best,
James Caldwell

emails.subject: Reminder: DOI Audit Prep – Case File Checklist Due May 30
emails.body_text:
Team,

As a reminder, the NV DOI market conduct exam window opens June 1. Please complete the case file checklist for all closed cases from January through April 2026 by May 30.

The checklist template is in SharePoint > Compliance > 2026 DOI Exam Prep. Key items:
  - Acknowledgment letter date vs. receipt date (must be ≤5 BD)
  - Decision letter date vs. receipt date (standard: ≤30 days; expedited: ≤72 hours)
  - External review forwarding timeliness (≤5 BD of member request)
  - Case file completeness (clinical memo, policy citation, member notice)

Please reach out if you have questions.

Kendel Dufree
Compliance Officer

emails.subject: Case 689 – Canyon Ridge Decision – Next Steps?
emails.body_text:
Ari,

We received the Canyon Ridge external review decision upholding the denial for Mr. Haines. We understand this closes the external review process.

For our records, can you confirm whether any further administrative appeals are available under the plan, or whether the next step would be to pursue legal or DOI complaint channels?

Thank you,
Carson Law Group

emails.subject: CASE-2026-00695 – Canyon Ridge Acknowledgment
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Canyon Ridge Review Services acknowledges receipt of the external review request for Case #CASE-2026-00695 (Member: D.O., CRR ref: CRR-2026-05-1204). Secure file transfer received.

Expected decision: within 45 calendar days (by June 18, 2026).

Canyon Ridge Review Services Intake Team

emails.subject: Lunch order today – who's in?
emails.body_text:

Ordering from Cactus Kitchen today, want in? I'm placing the order at 12:15. Text me or reply here.

Priya

emails.subject: Vickers (710) – Wife called re: May 17 review
emails.body_text:
Ari,

Renee Vickers called the main line asking what the outcome of the May 17 concurrent care review will mean for Noah. She's worried he'll be discharged before he's ready again. I told her Helena's review on the 17th will determine if continued stay is supported, and that we'll notify them within 24 hours of the determination. I told her to also ask Desert Valley's care coordinator to prepare updated progress notes.

Just keeping you in the loop. Might be worth a quick call to the family.

Darnell
